name: Green Chili with Pork
recipeIngredient: 5 cups Chicken stock or canned broth
28 ounces Crushed Italian plum tomatoes – drained
12 Large Poblano chilies (1.5 lb), roasted and peeled OR
28 ounces Can whole roasted mild green chilies – drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy duty casserole or Dutch oven (about 5 quarts), warm the oil over medium heat.
2. Add onions, garlic, Jalapenos, and carrots. Cook, stirring once or twice, for 10 minutes.
3. Stir in oregano and pork cubes. Cook until pork has lost its pink color, about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in chicken stock, salt (1 teaspoon), crushed tomatoes, and grated potato. Bring to a boil.
5. Lower the heat and partially cover; cook for 1 hour and 30 minutes, stirring often.
6. Cut Poblano peppers into half-inch strips. Add them to the chili and continue cooking, stirring frequently, until pork is tender and the chili thickens to your liking (30-45 minutes).
7. Taste for correct seasonings and let cook another 5 minutes before serving hot.
Note: To roast Poblanos, place them on a serving fork and turn over a gas burner until thoroughly charred. Wrap chilies in a paper bag after roasting. When cool, rinse under cold running water to remove the burned skin, pat dry, and de-stem before use.
